name: Open Interpreter
description: Open-source implementation of ChatGPT's Code Interpreter that runs locally. Lets LLMs execute Python, JavaScript, and shell commands on your machine via a natural language interface.
curator_note: The most accessible local code-execution agent — bridges the gap between LLMs and your filesystem.

name: AutoGPT
description: One of the first autonomous LLM agent frameworks. Chains GPT-4 calls with memory, web search, and file I/O to pursue long-horizon goals with minimal human intervention.
curator_note: Sparked the autonomous-agent wave in 2023; still the most starred AI agent repo on GitHub.

name: GPT Engineer
description: Agentic coding tool that generates an entire codebase from a single natural language prompt. Clarifies requirements interactively before writing and can modify existing projects.
curator_note: The canonical 'spec-to-codebase' project — great for rapid prototyping full applications.

name: SWE-agent
description: Princeton NLP's agent that autonomously resolves real GitHub issues on the SWE-bench benchmark. Uses a custom Agent-Computer Interface (ACI) optimized for code editing tasks.
curator_note: State-of-the-art on SWE-bench; defines the research frontier for autonomous software engineering.

name: Aider
description: AI pair programming tool for the terminal. Edits code across multiple files guided by natural language, with git integration that commits changes automatically with descriptive messages.
curator_note: Best terminal-native coding agent — git-aware edits and solid multi-file reasoning make it production-ready.
